Superseded by wm-state respawn refactor (dotfiles-ciw)

Split pre-clean into isolate-target (rename only) + kill-old-terminals (run after spawn). Restore now: rename target -> -old (phase 1), focus + append_layout + exec new attaches (per ws), sleep 600ms, kill old terminals on -old (phase 2), return to start_ws. Old tmux sessions stay alive across the cutover because their old client survives until the new client has attached.

GUI leaves now restored via mark+move. build-respawn-container emits placeholder con per GUI leaf with marks: [wm-gui-<ws>-<id>] + class swallow. main restore phase 3 (after kill-old-terminals): for each GUI spec, find live wid by saved-wid -> exact title -> title prefix -> class first-of, then [id=<wid>] floating disable, move container to mark <mark>; [con_mark=<mark>] kill. Stale wm-gui-* anchors cleaned at start of restore.

Built vanilla st 0.9.3 with kitty-style config (Iosevka Nerd Font Mono pixelsize=16, TokyoNight Night colors, borderpx=0). Installed to ~/.local/bin/st. Source at ~/repos/suckless/st. Patches deferred.

Built st-flexipatch w/ BOXDRAW, HIDECURSOR, LIGATURES, SIXEL, UNDERCURL, WIDE_GLYPHS. Note: kitty graphics protocol not in flexipatch — using SIXEL only. Source at ~/repos/suckless/st (vanilla backed up to st-vanilla.bak). Installed ~/.local/bin/st. Deps: harfbuzz, imlib2, libXft (all preinstalled).

Created dotfiles/st/ with config.h, patches.h, config.mk + dot.yaml. Symlinks active to ~/repos/suckless/st via rotz link st. Perf wins applied: -O3 -march=native -flto=auto, minlatency=8, SYNC_PATCH=1, scroll='scroll'. Built suckless scroll prog (~/.local/bin/scroll) for TTY buffering. Added st to meta-linux + meta-proot.
Patched launcherShow() to retrigger pathScanner. Resets _appBuffer + sets running=true on each show. Quickshell restarted to apply.
Verified live: 3 parallel wiki_preview + 1 wiki_references all return correctly. Hover bypass works, no timeouts.

Shipped: focus-dim overlay base (X11 GTK cairo + Wayland layer-shell). 9 tasks closed. Stories: n/a (no product/stories.yaml). Retro: parallel scrum-master flow works; main friction is worktree-base lag + reviewer interactive pause — addressable via dispatch prompt wording.

Shipped: focus-dim dialogs-bright follow-up. Floating + qs- titled windows now focusable cut-outs; rofi removed from ignore lists. 3 tasks closed. Stories: n/a. Retro: scrum-master flow smoother this round — explicit autonomous-merge wording in reviewer prompts worked; worktree-base lag still present, agents handle via rebase. Visual verification confirmed by user post-merge ('seems ok').

Added markdown_oxide via nvim-lspconfig opts in nvim/plugins/lang-md.lua. mason=false uses system /usr/sbin/markdown-oxide. root_markers=.moxide.toml/.obsidian scopes attach to vault. Verified headless: client attaches with root=/home/jan/.dotfiles/wiki, settings loaded, vault indexed.
Implemented marker walk (.moxide.toml, .obsidian) up then bounded-depth down from cwd. WIKI_ROOT env still wins if set. Fallback ~/.dotfiles/wiki. Verified detection across 6 cwds.
claude/dot.yaml installs cargo install markdown-oxide if missing + idempotent claude mcp add wiki-oxide at user scope. Verified guards match current state (skip).
Shipped: perles tool installed via claude/perles/ rotz package, integrated in claude/repos-health.nu. Idea doc was empty stub but work landed ad-hoc (commits c8c735f and predecessors).
Out of scope — nvim editor surface has no observed pain; today's epic targets MCP-side F+G only. Refile separately if cache staleness shows up in editor.
Out of scope — marketplace plugin parity is editor-side polish, not part of MCP LSP restore. Refile separately if asymmetry becomes a real annoyance.
